canonical_value: (A) The sketch illustrates the Cenozoic weathering history of the Th u ringisches Schiefergebirge close to Hohen- leuben (Berga anticline): Tertiary peneplanation and kaolini- zation is followed by post-Pleistocene deep valley cutting and ‘‘bleaching’’ (organic matter oxidation) of black slates, (B) the outcrop consists of white/gray slates in the upper section, approximately $3\,\mathrm{m}$ in thickness.
surface_forms: (A) The sketch illustrates the Cenozoic weathering history of the Th u ringisches Schiefergebirge close to Hohen- leuben (Berga anticline): Tertiary peneplanation and kaolini- zation is followed by post-Pleistocene deep valley cutting and ‘‘bleaching’’ (organic matter oxidation) of black slates, (B) the outcrop consists of white/gray slates in the upper section, approximately $3\,\mathrm{m}$ in thickness.

normalized_value.text: (A) The sketch illustrates the Cenozoic weathering history of the Th u ringisches Schiefergebirge close to Hohen- leuben (Berga anticline): Tertiary peneplanation and kaolini- zation is followed by post-Pleistocene deep valley cutting and ‘‘bleaching’’ (organic matter oxidation) of black slates, (B) the outcrop consists of white/gray slates in the upper section, approximately $3\,\mathrm{m}$ in thickness.

canonical_value: What type of rock is found in the lower section of the outcrop?
surface_forms: What type of rock is found in the lower section of the outcrop?

normalized_value.target_text: What type of rock is found in the lower section of the outcrop?

canonical_value:
Caption:
(A) The sketch illustrates the Cenozoic weathering history of the Th u ringisches Schiefergebirge close to Hohen- leuben (Berga anticline): Tertiary peneplanation and kaolini- zation is followed by post-Pleistocene deep valley cutting and ‘‘bleaching’’ (organic matter oxidation) of black slates, (B) the outcrop consists of white/gray slates in the upper section, approximately $3\,\mathrm{m}$ in thickness. The lower section consists of dark-colored black slate, and (C) channel filling of kaolinized slate clasts. Such fillings were only found in the upper section of the outcrop (see(A)). The channel fillings indicate the approximate level of the peneplain surface.
Question:
What type of rock is found in the lower section of the outcrop?
surface_forms:
Caption:
(A) The sketch illustrates the Cenozoic weathering history of the Th u ringisches Schiefergebirge close to Hohen- leuben (Berga anticline): Tertiary peneplanation and kaolini- zation is followed by post-Pleistocene deep valley cutting and ‘‘bleaching’’ (organic matter oxidation) of black slates, (B) the outcrop consists of white/gray slates in the upper section, approximately $3\,\mathrm{m}$ in thickness. The lower section consists of dark-colored black slate, and (C) channel filling of kaolinized slate clasts. Such fillings were only found in the upper section of the outcrop (see(A)). The channel fillings indicate the approximate level of the peneplain surface.
Question:
What type of rock is found in the lower section of the outcrop?
